mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative in >55% of rate-cut months, or rolling Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 5.0 percent, labor market slack signals weakening consumer demand and rising recession probability, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in >60% of triggered instances, or the signal loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous quarterly declines in real GDP and housing starts indicate a synchronized contraction in aggregate demand and construction investment, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint decline condition fails to predict negative 60-day returns with an accuracy exceeding 52%, or the interaction term coefficient is statistically indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during a late-cycle expansion reflects robust manufacturing capacity utilization and corporate earnings growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal generates negative 20-day returns in >55% of observations during late-cycle phases, or the predictive coefficient reverses sign when regime filters are applied.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 5.0 percent, restrictive monetary policy erodes equity valuations and triggers negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in >60% of triggered months, or the threshold effect disappears in a walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a declining unemployment rate confirms a strengthening labor market that supports consumer spending and generates positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined signal fails to outperform a baseline buy-and-hold strategy over 20-day horizons, or the interaction effect yields a negative Sharpe ratio in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Increases in real personal spending reflect sustained household consumption growth that directly boosts corporate revenues, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are negative in >55% of months with rising spending, or the rolling correlation between spending growth and equity returns falls below 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in the index of industrial production exceeding 0.5 percent signals immediate supply-side weakness and earnings downgrades, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in >60% of triggered instances, or the threshold signal shows no predictive power in a 12-month forward testing window.
